hypothesis: Applying class-balancing strategies during training—specifically oversampling rare reaction templates and undersampling frequent ones to achieve a more uniform template frequency distribution—will disproportionately improve top-k accuracy for rare templates (frequency 1–10) without substantially degrading accuracy for common templates (frequency >10,000), thereby reducing the performance disparity between rare and common templates by at least 30% compared to unbalanced training on the same dataset, as measured across all three model architectures (LocalRetro, MEGAN, and RootAligned) on the full USPTO-retro test set.
